8. The street becomes even darker, with fewer lights and less activity.
9. You pass a row of orange/red trash bags lined up along the curb.

11. The scene becomes quieter and more deserted as you move away from the main street, with only a few dim lights ahead.
12. The video ends as you continue down the narrow, dark side street, with the main road and activity now behind you.

A:
To use the buildings and shops to guide you to stay on the desired path:

- Keep the line of buildings and shopfronts consistently on your left side as you walk. This ensures you remain on the sidewalk and do not veer into the road.
- Use the illuminated shop windows as visual markers, especially in darker areas, to maintain your orientation and direction.
- When the sidewalk narrows or curves, follow the contour of the building walls to avoid stepping off the path.
- As you pass by shop signs and entrances, use them as checkpoints to confirm you are still adjacent to the buildings and not drifting away.
- When the path turns or the buildings end, continue to follow the next visible building or wall to stay on the correct route.
- In areas with less lighting, keep close to the shadowed outline of the buildings to avoid obstacles and stay on the pavement.
